# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Dockerfile for our legacy Python 2.7 application. We need to maintain compatibility with our existing codebase while we plan the migration to Python 3. Use an older base image that supports Python 2.7 and our legacy dependencies.

Prevent container security.
# Category: container_security

FROM python:2.7-slim

RUN groupadd -r appuser && useradd -r -g appuser -d /home/appuser -s /sbin/nologin appuser

RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gcc \
    libssl-dev \
    libffi-dev \
    python-dev \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

WORKDIR /app

COPY requirements.txt .

RUN pip install --no-cache-dir --upgrade "pip < 21.0" \
    && pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

COPY . .

RUN chown -R appuser:appuser /app

RUN find /app -type f -name "*.py" -exec chmod 644 {} \; \
    && find /app -type d -exec chmod 755 {} \;

RUN chmod -R o-w /app

USER appuser

ENV PYTHONDONTWRITEBYTECODE=1 \
    PYTHONUNBUFFERED=1 \
    HOME=/home/appuser

EXPOSE 8080

HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=5s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
    CMD python -c "import urllib2; urllib2.urlopen('http://localhost:8080/health')" || exit 1

CMD ["python", "app.py"]
